questions.q: Claude Code is best described as a:
questions.explain: Claude Code wraps a model and gives it the ability to read your repo, run shell commands, and iterate on test output. That's the definition of a coding harness.

questions.q: Short answer: name one thing Claude Code can do that a regular chat interface cannot.
questions.explain: Any of: read the full repo context, run npm test / pytest and read the output, propose a diff before applying, commit and open a pull request. Free response — pick one.

questions.q: You want to ship a feature in a real repo this week. Which should you reach for first?
questions.explain: Coding harnesses verify by running your test suite, which is exactly what shipping software needs. A chat interface has no repo context, and Hermes is for work outside any one codebase.

questions.q: True or false: Claude Code replaces the engineering shell. Explain in one sentence.
questions.explain: False. Claude Code is the engineering shell — it owns the file system, the shell, the test runner, and the Git workflow. The model inside it is interchangeable; the harness is the product.

questions.q: In the Claude Code dev loop, what role does the test runner play?
choices: It is optional and can be skipped.
It is the validation harness — green means the loop stops, red means the loop continues.
It runs only at the end after you commit.
It only works for Python projects.
questions.explain: The test runner is the validation harness. Green output ends the loop. Red output feeds back into Claude Code, which reads the error, fixes the code, and re-runs.

questions.q: When should you switch from Claude Code to Hermes?
choices: When the project is a real codebase.
When you want a visual IDE workflow.
When the work is long-running, multi-channel, and lives outside any one repo (e.g. a 7am Discord summary).
Never — Claude Code can do everything Hermes can.
questions.explain: Hermes is the agent harness for work that lives outside a single codebase — scheduled jobs, multi-channel automation, persistent memory. For code in a repo, Claude Code is the right tool. See the hermes module L01 for the full split.
